content.full_text:
October 26, 1977

Mr. Theodore E. Gutman
Democratic Clubs of the
San Fernando Valley
14448 Victory Blvd.
Van Nuys, California 91401

Dear Mr. Gutman:

Thank you for your letter of September 16, 1977. The duties
and responsibilities of a committee treasurer are explained
in the enclosed informational material. If you haven't already
done so, you must amend the Statement of Organization, Form 410,
reflecting the change in treasurer.

Also enclosed is a campaign disclosure manual and forms which
should be of assistance to you. If you require additional
assistance you might contact the local election office in either
the city or county clerk's office. Do not hesitate to contact
me if I can be of further assistance.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

mak
Enclosures

DEMOCRATIC CLUBS of the SAN FERNANDO VALLEY
14444 Victory Blvd., Ven Nuys, Cal. 91401 INFORMATION PHONE: 986-2295

Ven Nuys, Sept.16,1977

California Fair Political Practices Commission
14th Street
Sacramento, Calif.

Ladies and Gentlemen:

The Democratic Clubs of the San Fernando Valley have made reports on their political operations to you during the last part of 1976, during which time we operated two major headquarters in the San Fernando Valley.

Since January 1977 we are operating a small headquarters in Ven Nuys (per above address). Our budget calls for expenditure of under $ 2,000.- during 1977 and at no time has the bank account exceeded $ 400.- (so far). Income consists of Club contributions and donations; a fundraiser is planned for October. Rent, utilities, phone and other headquarters expenses will consume all funds.

Having recently, taken over as treasurer - my home address is 4101 Stansbury Ave, Sherman Oaks, Calif. 91423- may I request pertinent information as to when you require reports and if you have special forms for them, please send some. If not, what information do you need?

Yours very truly

Theodore E.(Red) Gutman
